content: As I turned into a narrow alley, a strange shimmer caught my eye—a tear in the air itself. Curiosity outweighed caution, and I stepped through. Suddenly, cold metal pressed beneath me. I was lying on a surgical table, sterile instruments gleaming on a chair beside me. The room was silent except for the faint hum of unseen machines. Panic flickered, but I forced calm. Why was I here? Who had brought me? The questions pressed as I scanned the tools, wondering if they were meant to heal or harm. Time felt suspended—every second stretched thin. Then, a realization: this wasn’t random. Someone or something had pulled me into this moment for a reason. I needed to find a way out, but first, I had to understand what awaited beyond the edge of that rip in time.
